forehead poker app Android.response:

**Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker** - 
This app lets you see the cards in your hand when holding your phone up to your forehead
. It's available on Google Play and offers social poker gameplay with friends.

**Heads Up Poker** - 
Play Heads Up Texas Hold'em with a friend on one device; viewing your hand is easy by tapping and holding your finger on the cards
.

**Headsup Holdem Poker** -

.

The **Poker Face** app specifically matches what you're looking for if you want the forehead-viewing mechanic. The other two are excellent alternatives for shared-device poker games where players pass the phone around.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
Here are the best Android apps available on the Google Play Store for playing **Blind Man's Bluff** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker**):

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Overview:** This is a highly polished, single-player psychological card game built specifically around the rules of Blind Man's Bluff. 
* **Key Features:** 
  * You are dealt a card face-down that only your opponent can see, while your opponent's card is shown face-up.
  * Features three AI difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, and Hard) where the AI's "poker face" and betting patterns change.
  * Includes an "Expression Mind Game" mechanic where you can change your avatar's expressions (like smirking or sweating) to manipulate the AI's decisions.
  * Fully playable offline with no Wi-Fi or account registration required.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Overview:** A dedicated multiplayer mobile adaptation of the classic forehead card game.
* **Key Features:** 
  * Designed to be played with friends and family.
  * Focuses on psychological warfare, requiring you to read your opponents' expressions and betting habits to guess your own card.
  * Clean, straightforward interface optimized for quick casual matches.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Overview:** A lightweight, no-frills digital version of the game.
* **Key Features:**
  * Simulates a standard deck of playing cards for local multiplayer setups.
  * Ideal if you want a simple, lightweight utility to host a game of Blind Man's Bluff with a group of people in the same room.

forehead poker app Android.response:
If you are looking to play "forehead poker"—the classic party card game also known as **Indian Poker** or **Blind Man's Bluff** (where you hold a card to your forehead so everyone can see it except you)—here are the best real apps available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Best for:** Single-player psychological gameplay.
* **Description:** This is a dedicated digital adaptation of Blind Man's Bluff / Indian Poker. In this single-player game, you are dealt a card face-down that you cannot see, but you can see your AI opponent's card. You have to read the AI's "poker face" expressions, tells, and betting patterns to guess whether your card is higher or lower, and then decide to call, raise, fold, or go all-in. 

### 2. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Best for:** Local multiplayer with friends.
* **Description:** A straightforward, lightweight utility app designed specifically for playing the physical "card-on-forehead" game. It acts as a digital deck of cards for groups of people sitting together. You hold your phone to your forehead so your friends can see your card, and you use the app to facilitate the betting and revealing process without needing a physical deck of cards.

### 3. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
* **Best for:** The ultimate "phone-on-forehead" party experience.
* **Description:** If you are looking for the popular party game where you physically hold the phone to your forehead, this is the official app made famous by Ellen DeGeneres. While it is a word-guessing/charades game rather than a card-betting game, it perfectly utilizes the "forehead" mechanic. You hold the phone to your head, and your friends must shout clues to help you guess the word on the screen before the timer runs out.

### 4. **Teen Patti Gold** (by Moonfrog)
* **Best for:** Online multiplayer "Indian Poker" variations.
* **Description:** In India, the term "Indian Poker" is widely used to describe **Teen Patti** (a traditional 3-card poker game). If you are looking for this style of online multiplayer poker, *Teen Patti Gold* is one of the most popular options on Android. It features a variety of game modes, including "blind" betting options where you bet without looking at your cards.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
Here are several excellent Android apps available on the Google Play Store that feature card games where you cannot see your own cards:

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Description:** This is a dedicated single-player adaptation of **Blind Man's Bluff** (also widely known as **Indian Poker**). In this psychological card game, you are dealt a card that you cannot see, but your AI opponent can. Meanwhile, you can see the AI's card. You must read the AI's expressions, betting patterns, and tells to guess the value of your own card and decide whether to Call, Raise, Fold, or go All-In. 

### 2. **Boardible: Games for Groups**
* **Description:** Boardible is a multiplayer board game platform that features a fully licensed digital version of **Hanabi**, the critically acclaimed cooperative card game. In *Hanabi*, players represent distracted pyrotechnicians trying to launch a spectacular fireworks show. The twist is that you hold your cards facing outward—meaning you can see everyone else's cards, but you cannot see your own. You must rely on limited, strategic clues from your teammates to know which cards to play or discard.

### 3. **Indian Poker (by Kanbe)**
* **Description:** If you are looking for a simple, no-frills digital tool to play the classic "card-on-the-forehead" game with friends in person, this app simulates the traditional Indian Poker deck. It allows a group of players to gather around, receive cards they cannot see, and play the game using their mobile devices.

### 4. **Heads Up!**
* **Description:** While technically a word-guessing party game rather than a traditional playing card game, *Heads Up!* popularized the physical "card on the forehead" mechanic in mobile gaming. You hold your phone to your forehead facing your friends. A "card" is displayed on the screen that everyone else can see, but you cannot. Your friends must shout clues, act, or sing to help you guess what card you are holding before the timer runs out.

You tilt the phone down when you guess correctly and tilt it up to skip.

The best and most popular Android apps for this on the Google Play Store include:

### 1. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
This is the official, incredibly popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres.

Another excellent free option that mimics the classic party game.
* **Features:** It features over 60 active decks. It is highly customizable, allowing you to easily create your own custom charades decks and play in multiple languages.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some of the best real, offline, single-player bluffing games available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game** (by Hamilton Digital Media)
* **What it is:** A dedicated offline adaptation of the classic card game "Cheat" (also known as *Bluff*, *I Doubt It*, or *BS*). 
* **How it plays:** You play against three computer-controlled AI opponents. The goal is to get rid of all your cards by playing them face-down and declaring their rank. You can lie about what you are playing, but if the AI calls your bluff, you have to pick up the entire pile. 
* **Why it fits:** It is fully offline-friendly and features unique single-player mechanics like strategic power-ups (e.g., Lie Counters and Bluff Shields) to make playing against the CPU highly engaging.

### 2. **Bluff Card Game Offline** (by Vertex / Oneiric Gaming)
* **What it is:** A smooth, offline-first card game designed specifically to be played without an internet connection.
* **How it plays:** It offers both a quick 1v1 mode against a smart AI opponent and a 4-player full-table mode. It features clean card animations, daily offline quests, and difficulty settings that adjust how easily the AI can spot your bluffs.
* **Why it fits:** It requires absolutely no Wi-Fi or data, making it perfect for commutes or offline play.

### 3. **Liar's Dice** (by Hamilton Digital Media)
* **What it is:** While technically a dice-based game rather than a card game, *Liar's Dice* (also known as *Perudo* or *Dudo*) is one of the absolute best offline bluffing experiences on Android.
* **How it plays:** You roll your dice in secret and take turns making bids on how many dice of a certain face are on the table. You must bluff your way through bids or call the AI opponents "liars" when you think they are overbidding. 
* **Why it fits:** The game is built for fast, one-handed offline play and features computer opponents programmed with distinct "personalities" and bluffing styles.

### 4. **Liar's Dice Online** (by YoAmb)
* **What it is:** A highly-rated digital version of the classic pirate bluffing game.
* **How it plays:** Although the game features online multiplayer, it includes a robust, fully-featured **Offline Practice Mode**. 
* **Why it fits:** The offline mode utilizes an intelligent AI that acts as a great training ground for practicing your psychological bluffing and probability skills without needing an active internet connection.

best offline single player card game And.response:
If you are looking for the best offline, single-player card games on Android, the following highly-rated titles offer deep strategy, excellent replayability, and require absolutely no internet connection to play:

### 1. **Balatro**
* **Genre:** Poker Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Description:** One of the most addictive card games available, *Balatro* blends traditional poker hands with game-changing Joker cards. Your goal is to score enough chips to beat "Boss Blinds" by discovering wild combos, upgrading your cards, and unlocking new decks. It is a premium game that runs flawlessly offline and is perfect for quick sessions or hours-long deep dives.

### 2. **Slay the Spire**
* **Genre:** Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Description:** The gold standard of the roguelike deckbuilding genre. You choose one of four distinct characters, each with their own unique pool of cards, and fight your way up a procedurally generated spire. The balance, synergy-building, and tactical decision-making are unmatched, making it endlessly replayable without needing an internet connection.

### 3. **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
* **Genre:** Dark Fantasy RPG Deckbuilder
* **Description:** Unlike many mobile games, *Dawncaster* was built specifically for mobile devices and is played in portrait mode. It features a rich dark-fantasy atmosphere where you choose a class (like Warrior, Rogue, or Arcanist) and build a deck to defeat challenging monsters. It is a premium game with no ads or pay-to-win mechanics, making it a pure offline RPG experience.

### 4. **Pirates Outlaws**
* **Genre:** Pirate-Themed Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Description:** If you like *Slay the Spire* but want a different aesthetic, *Pirates Outlaws* is an excellent choice. You navigate dangerous seas, manage your ammo (which acts as your mana), and battle outlaws. It features over 16 unique heroes, 700+ cards, and multiple maps to explore. 

### 5. **Night of the Full Moon**
* **Genre:** Dark Fairy-Tale Deckbuilder
* **Description:** Based on the story of Little Red Riding Hood, this game tasks you with venturing into the Black Forest to find your missing grandmother. It features a beautiful, slightly eerie art style, multiple character classes, and a highly strategic turn-based card combat system. It is free to try with optional purchases for additional classes and story chapters.

### 6. **Card Thief**
* **Genre:** Stealth Puzzle Card Game
* **Description:** For something a bit different from traditional deckbuilders, *Card Thief* presents stealth gameplay on a 3x3 grid of cards. You play as a thief sneaking through the shadows, avoiding guards, extinguishing torches, and stealing treasure. It is a brilliant, tactical puzzle game that is easy to play in short bursts offline.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
The specific Android app for **Blind Man's Bluff** (also known as **Indian Poker**) is called **Blindex**.

**Blindex** is a free, offline, single-player card game where you see your opponent's card but never your own, exactly matching the rules of Blind Man's Bluff [1][2].

### Key Features of Blindex:
| Feature | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Game Type** | Single-player psychological card game against an AI dealer [1] |
| **Offline Play** | 100% offline; no Wi-Fi or internet connection required [1][2] |
| **Cost** | 100% free with **no ads** and **no in-app purchases** [1] |
| **Difficulty** | Three difficulty levels with different AI bluffing styles [1] |
| **Customization** | Earn points to buy cosmetic hairstyles, outfits, card decks, and tables [1] |
| **Languages** | Supports English and Korean (auto-detects device language) [2] |

You can download it directly from the **Google Play Store** under the name **Blindex - Blind Card Game** [2].

*Note: Many other apps on Google Play titled simply "Bluff" (e.g., by Magic Board, Floating Ninja Games, or Oneiric Gaming) refer to a different game where the goal is to get rid of cards by lying about their value (also known as "Cheat" or "I Doubt It"), not Blind Man's Bluff [4][5][6].*

| App Name | Developer | Key Feature |

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The card game you are describing is most likely **Heads Up!** (or a variation like **Heads!**), where players hold a card on their forehead that **everyone else can see but they cannot**, and they must guess the card based on others' clues.

However, if you are looking for a specific **Android app** that implements this "invisible to self" mechanic as a digital game, the most direct match is **Heads Up!** by **Vine Games** (often found as "Heads Up!" or "Heads!").

Here are the best Android apps for this specific mechanic:

| App Name | Developer | How it works |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Heads Up!** | Vine Games | Players hold the phone to their forehead; the card is visible to everyone else but hidden from the holder until they guess or pass [5]. |
| **Heads!** | Various (e.g., "Heads! Card Game") | Similar to Heads Up!, often includes custom card sets and party modes where you can't see your own card [5]. |
| **MiniCards — Card games** | Various | A collection including **Hearts**, **Canasta**, and **Snap**; some modes in these classic games involve hidden hands, though not always "invisible to self" [5]. |
| **Rummy - offline card game** | LITE Games | Allows you to **hide your cards** or place them on the table, but typically you can still see your own hand unless using a specific "reverse" mode in a simulator [2]. |

**Important Note:**
Most standard digital card games (like Poker, Rummy, or Hearts) hide your hand from *other players*, not from *you*. The "can't see your own card" mechanic is almost exclusively a **physical party game** (like Heads Up!) that has been digitized.

If you are looking for a **digital simulator** to play physical games like this with friends online where you can't see your own card, **Tabletop Simulator** (via Steam, not Android) or **VirtualTableTop.io** (web-based) offer a "Reverse" or "Blind" mode where cards in a player's hand are visible to everyone else but not to the player themselves [3][6].

For a pure Android app experience of this specific mechanic, **Heads Up!** is the standard recommendation.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The Android card game you’re describing is **Heads Up!**, where one player holds the phone to their forehead with the screen facing outward, and friends give clues to help them guess the word displayed [1][3].

**Key details:**
- **How it works**: The player picks a themed card deck (e.g., animals, celebrities, accents), holds the phone to their forehead like a headband, and guesses words based on friends’ clues before a 60-second timer runs out [1][2].
- **Controls**: Tilt the phone downward to confirm a correct guess, or upward to pass to the next word [4][7].
- **Popularity**: The game gained fame through Ellen DeGeneres, who plays it with guests on her show [3][15].
- **Alternative apps**: Similar games include **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** and **For Your Head**, which follow the same forehead-holding charades style [2][12].

If you’re looking for the original and most popular version, download **Heads Up!** from the Google Play Store [1].

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
**Hanabi Tracker** (Google Play): A handy utility app for playing the cooperative card game Hanabi, where each player sees everyone else's cards but not their own—you give hints and track clues to build fireworks sequences as a team.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.rpalazzo.hanabitracker&hl=en_US)

**Hanabi** (various Android implementations, e.g., via APK sources like the one by grootkoerkamp): Digital version of the award-winning game with the core "can't see your own cards" mechanic.[[2]](https://apkpure.com/hanabi/com.grootkoerkamp.games.hanabi.app)

Search the Play Store for "Hanabi" for any current official or updated releases, as digital adaptations are often available or playable via browser platforms like Board Game Arena.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
**Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros.) is the perfect match. This popular Android party game app has players hold their phone to their forehead like a card while teammates give clues to guess the word or phrase displayed on screen before the timer runs out. It features various themed decks (movies, celebrities, animals, etc.) and is available on the Google Play Store.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.wb.headsup&hl=en_US)

Other similar guessing/party apps exist (like charades-style games), but this one directly replicates the "hold to forehead" mechanic from the classic Ellen DeGeneres version.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
**Bluff Card Game** (by oEngines or similar developers on Google Play) – The perfect match: a classic single-player bluffing card game (also called Cheat, BS, or I Doubt It) with smart AI opponents. Fully offline, no internet required, focused on deception, memory, and strategy as you try to shed cards while calling bluffs.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.oengines.bluffoffline&hl=en_US)

**Bluff** – Another solid Android option for the same bluffing card game mechanics, with single-player/AI support and offline play available.[[2]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=bluff.card.game&hl=en_US)

For a poker-style bluffing experience, try dedicated offline Texas Hold'em apps like **Poker Offline** or **Texas Holdem Poker Offline** (search Google Play for these exact titles)—they feature single-player modes against AI that bluff and fold realistically. All are real, free/paid apps available on the Google Play Store.

best offline single player card game And.response:
**Top recommendations for best offline single-player card games on Android (Google Play):**

- **Slay the Spire** — The definitive roguelike deck-builder where you build powerful card combos, fight enemies, and climb a tower in procedurally generated runs. Fully offline and endlessly replayable.[[1]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9tV6UbDeWsY)

- **Balatro** — Addictive poker roguelike that twists classic poker hands with jokers, modifiers, and roguelike progression for deep strategy and high scores. Excellent offline single-player experience.[[2]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fAI47CycaMU)

- **Pirates Outlaws** — Fun pirate-themed deck-building roguelike with card collection, upgrades, and challenging runs against enemies. Strong single-player focus and works completely offline.[[3]](https://www.reddit.com/r/AndroidGaming/comments/17s4lls/looking_for_cool_single_player_card_gamescan_be/)

- **Card Thief** — Clever stealth puzzle card game where you plan heists by moving through a grid of cards, avoiding guards, and stealing loot. Short, satisfying sessions.[[4]](https://minireview.io/top-mobile-games/best-card-games-mobile)

- **Ultimate Offline Card Games** — All-in-one app with classic single-player modes for Solitaire, Rummy 500, Canasta, Hearts, Spades (solo variants), and more. No internet required.[[5]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.eastudios.cardsclub&hl=en_US)

These are highly rated, truly offline, and focused on solo play. Slay the Spire and Balatro stand out as the most acclaimed modern options.
